question: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest age-adjusted death rate for all races and both sexes in the United States? Your response should be in the format [Answer].
practical_motivation: A health policy analyst wants the year with the lowest overall death rate in the early 2010s.

1.fact: Filtering to Race = "All Races" and Sex = "Both Sexes" for 2011-2016, the minimum Age-adjusted Death Rate is 724.6 in 2014.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest age-adjusted death rate for all races and both sexes in the United States? (Filter Race == "All Races" and Sex == "Both Sexes"; select the minimum Age-adjusted Death Rate.)

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Which year from 2011-2016 has the lowest age-adjusted death rate for all races and both sexes? (Filter Race == "All Races" and Sex == "Both Sexes"; select the minimum Age-adjusted Death Rate.) The answer is 2014.
